mislead_scenario: An agent tasked with a straightforward but multi-component bug (e.g., adding a missing DB index to speed up an API) invokes this skill, falls into analysis paralysis, and wastes thousands of tokens drawing broken ASCII causal loop diagrams and philosophizing about 'paradigm shifts' instead of writing the one-line SQL fix.
missing_boundaries: Fails to establish a complexity floor—agents might apply this to standard CRUD tasks just because they involve a client, API, and DB.
Does not tell the agent when to STOP mapping the system and start executing the fix.
Lacks distinction from 'thinking-feedback-loops' and 'eng-holistic-systems', likely causing redundant skill invocations.
weakest_part: The ASCII Causal Loop Diagram template. Enforcing complex ASCII art generation on an LLM is brittle, token-heavy, prone to formatting errors, and provides zero semantic reasoning lift compared to a simple bulleted list of relationships.
highest_leverage_fix: Remove the ASCII diagram templates entirely and replace them with a text-based, structured relationship mapping protocol. Add a strict boundary explicitly instructing the agent NOT to use this for deterministic, localized bugs.
